question.text: What is the minimum tendering period for an open offer under SAST?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The tendering period under SAST must be not less than 10 working days. This gives shareholders adequate time to evaluate the offer and decide whether to tender their shares. **The Trap:** 15 working days is the window for competing offers, not the minimum tendering period. 21 calendar days may be confused with other regulatory timelines under Companies Act. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests working day calculations. Note that "working days" excludes weekends and stock exchange holidays, so 10 working days typically spans about 14 calendar days. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise clients not to rush into tendering on day one. Use the full tendering period to assess whether a competing offer might emerge (within 15 working days of DPS) and whether the offer price is fair.
